message: .map().filter() iterates the array twice — combine into a single loop with .reduce() or for...of
help: Combine `.map().filter()` (or similar chains) into a single pass with `.reduce()` or a `for...of` loop to avoid iterating the array twice

message: .map().filter() iterates the array twice — combine into a single loop with .reduce() or for...of

message: useContext is superseded by `use()` on React 19+ — `use()` reads context conditionally inside hooks, branches, and loops; switch to `import { use } from 'react'`
help: Pass `ref` as a regular prop on function components — `forwardRef` is no longer needed in React 19+. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)` for branch-aware context reads. Only enabled on projects detected as React 19+.

message: await inside a for…of loop runs the calls sequentially — for independent operations, collect them and use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` to run them concurrently
help: Collect the items and use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` to run independent operations concurrently

message: await inside a for-loop runs the calls sequentially — for independent operations, collect them and use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` to run them concurrently
help: Collect the items and use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` to run independent operations concurrently

message: array.includes() in a loop is O(n) per call — convert to a Set for O(1) lookups
help: Use a `Set` or `Map` for repeated membership tests / keyed lookups — `Array.includes`/`find` is O(n) per call

message: array.find() in a loop is O(n*m) — build a Map for O(1) lookups
help: Build an index `Map` once outside the loop instead of `array.find(...)` inside it

message: Three-period ellipsis ("...") in JSX text — use the actual ellipsis character "…" (or `…`)
help: Use the typographic ellipsis "…" (or `…`) instead of three periods — pairs with action-with-followup labels ("Rename…", "Loading…")

message: Array index "i" used as key — causes bugs when list is reordered or filtered
help: Use a stable unique identifier: `key={item.id}` or `key={item.slug}` — index keys break on reorder/filter

message: useMutation without a cache update — stale data may remain after the mutation. Call queryClient.invalidateQueries / setQueryData / resetQueries / refetchQueries inside onSuccess (or trigger a router refresh)
help: Add `onSuccess: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['...'] })` so cached data stays in sync after the mutation

message: useState(selector()) calls initializer on every render — use useState(() => selector()) for lazy initialization

message: useMutation without a cache update — stale data may remain after the mutation. Call queryClient.invalidateQueries / setQueryData / resetQueries / refetchQueries inside onSuccess (or trigger a router refresh)
help: Add `onSuccess: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['...'] })` so cached data stays in sync after the mutation

message: new RegExp() inside a loop — hoist to a module-level constant
help: Hoist `new RegExp(...)` (or large regex literals) to a module-level constant so it isn't recompiled on every loop iteration

message: .every() over an array compared to another array — short-circuit with `a.length === b.length && a.every(...)` so unequal-length arrays exit immediately
help: Short-circuit with `a.length === b.length && a.every((x, i) => x === b[i])` — unequal-length arrays exit immediately

message: .every() over an array compared to another array — short-circuit with `a.length === b.length && a.every(...)` so unequal-length arrays exit immediately

message: "touchstart" listener without { passive: true } — blocks scrolling performance. Only add { passive: true } if the handler does NOT call event.preventDefault() (passive listeners silently ignore preventDefault())
help: Add `{ passive: true }` as the third argument: `addEventListener('scroll', handler, { passive: true })`. Only do this if the handler does NOT call `event.preventDefault()` — passive listeners silently ignore `preventDefault()`, which breaks features like pull-to-refresh suppression, custom gestures, and nested-scroll containment.

message: "touchend" listener without { passive: true } — blocks scrolling performance. Only add { passive: true } if the handler does NOT call event.preventDefault() (passive listeners silently ignore preventDefault())
help: Add `{ passive: true }` as the third argument: `addEventListener('scroll', handler, { passive: true })`. Only do this if the handler does NOT call `event.preventDefault()` — passive listeners silently ignore `preventDefault()`, which breaks features like pull-to-refresh suppression, custom gestures, and nested-scroll containment.
